meta.title: N5 Vocabulary: Basics
meta.subject: japanese
meta.subtopic: Greetings, Pronouns, Time Expressions & Essential Phrases
meta.description: Master the 30 most essential Japanese words for N5 — greetings, pronouns, and everyday expressions.

items.back:
Japanese uses three writing systems:
• ひらがな (hiragana) — phonetic syllables for Japanese words
• カタカナ (katakana) — phonetic syllables for foreign words
• 漢字 (kanji) — characters borrowed from Chinese

N5 is the beginner level of the JLPT exam.
items.hint: You'll learn all three writing systems as you progress!

items.back:
🌞 Hello / Good afternoon

Used from around 10 AM to 5 PM.

More formal: こんにちは (always works)
Casual: やあ (yaa) or ねえ (nee) among friends

Example:
A: こんにちは！
B: こんにちは！お元気ですか？
    (How are you?)
items.hint: The most universal Japanese greeting!

items.front: Japanese Greetings by Time of Day
items.back:
☀️ おはようございます — Good morning
   (ohayou gozaimasu) — formal
   おはよう (ohayou) — casual

🌞 こんにちは — Hello / Good afternoon
   (konnichiwa)

🌙 こんばんは — Good evening
   (konbanwa)

🛌 おやすみなさい — Good night
   (oyasumi nasai)
   おやすみ (oyasumi) — casual
items.hint: Match your greeting to the time of day!

items.front:
ありがとうございます
(arigatou gozaimasu)
items.back:
🙏 Thank you (formal / polite)

Casual: ありがとう (arigatou)
Very casual: どうも (doumo)

Responding to thanks:
• どういたしまして (dou itashimashite) — You're welcome
• いえいえ (ie ie) — Not at all

Example:
「ありがとうございます！」
「どういたしまして。」
items.hint: Always use ございます with teachers or strangers.

items.back:
👤 First person (I / me):
• わたし / 私 (watashi) — polite
• ぼく / 僕 (boku) — casual male
• あたし (atashi) — casual female

👥 Second person (you):
• あなた (anata) — polite (avoid overusing)

👨 Third person:
• かれ / 彼 (kare) — he / boyfriend
• かのじょ / 彼女 (kanojo) — she / girlfriend

Tip: Japanese often omits pronouns when the subject is clear from context!
items.hint: Japanese speakers often drop the subject when it's obvious.

items.back:
📅 Key time expressions:

• きょう / 今日 (kyou) — today
• あした / 明日 (ashita) — tomorrow
• きのう / 昨日 (kinou) — yesterday
• いま / 今 (ima) — now
• まいにち / 毎日 (mainichi) — every day

Parts of the day:
• あさ / 朝 (asa) — morning
• ひる / 昼 (hiru) — noon / daytime
• よる / 夜 (yoru) — night
items.hint: Memorise きょう, あした, きのう as a trio!

items.back:
✅ はい (hai) — Yes
❌ いいえ (iie) — No

Polite ways to decline:
• いいえ、けっこうです (iie, kekkou desu) — No, thank you
• ちょっと… (chotto…) — A bit difficult… (softer refusal)

Note: In Japanese culture, direct refusal is often avoided. ちょっと (a little / somewhat) is commonly used to politely say no.
items.hint: いいえ can sound blunt — ちょっと is a softer 'no'.

items.front:
すみません & おねがいします
(sumimasen & onegaishimasu)
items.back:
🙇 すみません (sumimasen)
• Excuse me (to get attention)
• I'm sorry (mild apology)
• Used before asking for help

🙏 おねがいします (onegaishimasu)
• Please (requesting something)
• More polite than ください (kudasai)

Example:
すみません、水をおねがいします。
(Excuse me, please give me water.)
items.hint: すみません + おねがいします is a perfect polite request combo!

items.question: こんにちは (konnichiwa)
items.hint: Used during daytime — the most common greeting

items.question: おはようございます (ohayou gozaimasu)
items.hint: The ございます form is polite — say おはよう with friends

items.question: こんばんは (konbanwa)
items.hint: Used after sunset

items.question: おやすみなさい (oyasumi nasai)
items.hint: Said when going to sleep

items.question: さようなら (sayounara)
items.hint: A formal goodbye — implies you may not see them for a while

items.question: ありがとうございます (arigatou gozaimasu)
items.hint: The most polite form of 'thank you'

items.question: すみません (sumimasen)
items.hint: Use it to get someone's attention or apologise gently

items.question: はじめまして (hajimemashite)
items.hint: Said when meeting someone for the first time

items.question: はい (hai)
items.hint: Simple affirmative — can also show you are listening

items.question: いいえ (iie)
items.hint: Polite negative — Japanese often avoid saying this directly

items.question: おねがいします (onegaishimasu)
items.hint: Used when requesting something — more polite than ください

items.question: どうぞ (douzo)
items.answer: please go ahead / here you are
options: please go ahead / here you are
items.hint: Said when offering something or giving someone permission

items.question: わかりました (wakarimashita)
items.hint: Confirming you have understood something

items.question: きょう / 今日 (kyou)
items.hint: 今 = now + 日 = day

items.question: あした / 明日 (ashita)
items.hint: 明 (bright) + 日 (day) = the bright next day

items.question: きのう / 昨日 (kinou)
items.hint: The day that has already passed

items.question: いま / 今 (ima)
items.hint: This very moment

items.question: まいにち / 毎日 (mainichi)
items.hint: 毎 = every + 日 = day

items.question: あさ / 朝 (asa)
items.hint: Start of the day — おはよう is said during this time

items.question: ひる / 昼 (hiru)
items.hint: Middle of the day

items.question: よる / 夜 (yoru)
items.hint: こんばんは is used at the start of this time

items.question: わたし / 私 (watashi)
items.hint: The polite, gender-neutral first-person pronoun

items.question: あなた (anata)
items.hint: Polite 'you' — in conversation, names are often used instead

items.question: かれ / 彼 (kare)
items.hint: Also means 'boyfriend'

items.question: かのじょ / 彼女 (kanojo)
items.hint: Also means 'girlfriend'

items.question: わたしたち / 私たち (watashitachi)
items.hint: たち is added to make pronouns plural

items.question: みなさん (minasan)
items.hint: Used to address a group: みなさん、おはようございます！

items.question: ともだち / 友達 (tomodachi)
items.hint: 友 = friendship, 達 = people (plural)

items.question: せんせい / 先生 (sensei)
items.hint: Also used for doctors, masters, experts

items.question: がくせい / 学生 (gakusei)
items.hint: 学 = study, 生 = life/person

items.question: おはようございます (ohayou gozaimasu)
ありがとうございます (arigatou gozaimasu)
どうぞ (douzo)
items.answer: please go ahead / here you are
options: please go ahead / here you are

items.question: わたしたち / 私たち (watashitachi)
